segments.text: Korah son of Izhar, the son of Kohath, the son of Levi, and certain Reubenites—Dathan and Abiram, sons of Eliab, and On son of Peleth—became insolent
and rose up against Moses. With them were 250 Israelite men, well-known community leaders who had been appointed members of the council.
They came as a group to oppose Moses and Aaron and said to them,
You have gone too far! The whole community is holy, every one of them, and the Lord is with them. Why then do you set yourselves above the Lord's assembly?
segments.speaker: Korah and followers

segments.text: When Moses heard this, he fell facedown.
Then he said to Korah and all his followers:
In the morning the Lord will show who belongs to him and who is holy, and he will have that person come near him. The man he chooses he will cause to come near him.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: You, Korah, and all your followers are to do this: Take censers
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: and tomorrow put burning coals and incense in them before the Lord. The man the Lord chooses will be the one who is holy. You Levites have gone too far!
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Moses also said to Korah,
Now listen, you Levites!
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Isn't it enough for you that the God of Israel has separated you from the rest of the Israelite community and brought you near himself to do the work at the Lord's tabernacle and to stand before the community and minister to them?
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: He has brought you and all your fellow Levites near himself, but now you are trying to get the priesthood too.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: It is against the Lord that you and all your followers have banded together. Who is Aaron that you should grumble against him?
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Then Moses summoned Dathan and Abiram, the sons of Eliab. But they said,
We will not come!
segments.speaker: Dathan and Abiram

segments.text: Isn't it enough that you have brought us up out of a land flowing with milk and honey to kill us in the wilderness? And now you also want to lord it over us!
segments.speaker: Dathan and Abiram

segments.text: Moreover, you haven't brought us into a land flowing with milk and honey or given us an inheritance of fields and vineyards. Do you want to treat these men like slaves? No, we will not come!
segments.speaker: Dathan and Abiram

segments.text: Then Moses became very angry and said to the Lord,
Do not accept their offering. I have not taken so much as a donkey from them, nor have I wronged any of them.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Moses said to Korah,
You and all your followers are to appear before the Lord tomorrow—you and they and Aaron.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Each man is to take his censer and put incense in it—250 censers in all—and present it before the Lord. You and Aaron are to present your censers also.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: The Lord said to Moses and Aaron,
Separate yourselves from this assembly so I can put an end to them at once.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: But Moses and Aaron fell facedown and cried out,
O God, the God who gives breath to all living things, will you be angry with the entire assembly when only one man sins?
segments.speaker: Moses and Aaron

segments.text: Then the Lord said to Moses,
Say to the assembly, 'Move away from the tents of Korah, Dathan and Abiram.'
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then Moses said,
This is how you will know that the Lord has sent me to do all these things and that it was not my idea:
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: If these men die a natural death and suffer the fate of all mankind, then the Lord has not sent me.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: But if the Lord brings about something totally new, and the earth opens its mouth and swallows them, with everything that belongs to them, and they go down alive into the realm of the dead, then you will know that these men have treated the Lord with contempt.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: The Lord said to Moses,
Tell Eleazar son of Aaron, the priest, to remove the censers from the charred remains and scatter the coals some distance away, for the censers are holy—
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The next day the whole Israelite community grumbled against Moses and Aaron.
You have killed the Lord's people
segments.speaker: Israelite community

segments.text: and the Lord said to Moses,
Get away from this assembly so I can put an end to them at once.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then Moses said to Aaron,
Take your censer and put incense in it, along with burning coals from the altar, and hurry to the assembly to make atonement for them. Wrath has come out from the Lord; the plague has started.
segments.speaker: Moses
